examples: The combination of rising inflation and slowing growth may bear a worrying resemblance to the stagflationary 1970s, but there has been a sea-change in advanced economies since then.
The data never supported that theory, and the stagflationary 1970s totally destroyed the validity and credibility of the Phillips curve.
Moreover, some experts argue that there has been no significant shock to the economy that might push the U.S. into a stagflationary mode.
The lows for the ratio came in the stagflationary 1970s.
